findings.message: Layering violation: route handler directly opens a database session via SessionLocal() instead of using the repository layer. The equivalent create_item(session, name) function already exists in ../db/repository.py but is bypassed. This couples the route directly to the database engine, defeats dependency injection, and inconsistently handles session lifecycle (doesn't commit, unlike the repository version).
findings.suggestion: Use FastAPI dependency injection: change the handler to accept 'session: SessionDep' and call 'create_item(session, payload.name)' from the repository layer instead of managing SessionLocal() directly. This matches the pattern used in get_items and ensures consistent session management across the application.
